title: Electric Shock - Recognition and Immediate Action
immediate_steps: Ensure the power supply is turned off or the source is safely disconnected first
Only approach the victim after ensuring safety
Check responsiveness and breathing immediately after ensuring safety
avoid: Do not touch them while they are still in contact with the electrical source

title: Electric Shock - Do's and Don'ts and Safety Steps
immediate_steps: Switch off the power source or unplug the device before touching the victim
If power cannot be switched off, use a dry non-conductive object such as wood or plastic to separate the person
Check breathing and pulse once the person is safe
avoid: Do not touch the victim directly while they are still in contact with electricity
Do not use wet objects or metal to separate them
Do not attempt to move the person before ensuring the area is safe

title: Electric Shock - Injuries and Internal Effects
symptoms: Burns where electricity entered and exited
Fractures or dislocations from sudden movement
immediate_steps: Treat as a severe medical emergency as internal damage can affect muscles, nerves, and organs even if skin injuries appear minor

title: Electric Shock - Post Rescue Care and Monitoring
immediate_steps: Place the person in a recovery position if they are breathing but unconscious
Monitor continuously for signs of complications such as irregular heartbeat, breathing difficulty, burns, or confusion
Keep the person warm and comfortable
Ensure medical evaluation even if the person appears fine (internal injuries and rhythm disturbances may occur later)
